description_long: This chapel features a symmetrical facade with two prominent bell towers flanking the main entrance, each topped with a white spire and black trim. The central entrance is arched and adorned with classical columns, decorative moldings, and a small pediment, giving it a grand and inviting appearance. The roof is covered with reddish-brown tiles, and the walls are primarily a light cream color, accented by darker window frames and architectural details. The side walls are relatively plain, with small windows and minimal ornamentation, while the rear of the chapel is simple and unadorned. The structure sits on a black rectangular base, suggesting a foundation or platform. Overall, the design blends Baroque and Neo-Classical elements, with a focus on symmetry, verticality, and ornate detailing at the entrance.
description: A symmetrical chapel with two bell towers, a grand arched entrance, and a tiled roof, featuring ornate Baroque and Neo-Classical details in cream, black, and reddish-brown tones.
description_view_0: Side view showing the length of the chapel, the tiled roof, and the two bell towers rising above the structure.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the ornate arched entrance, symmetrical bell towers, and decorative facade details.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, displaying the plain side wall, tiled roof, and both bell towers.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the simple, unadorned back wall and the tops of the bell towers, with minimal decorative features.
attribute_steeple_or_bell_tower: The chapel has two bell towers, one on each side of the main entrance. Each tower is square, features black and white color accents, and is topped with a white spire.
attribute_entrance_door_design: The main entrance door is arched, made of wood, and painted in a warm brown tone. It is framed by classical columns, decorative moldings, and a small pediment, with additional ornamental details above the arch.
